'''Dorriella''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Journal 9" /> located in the [[Dorriella system/Legends|Dorriella system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Wazta sector/Legends|Wazta sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> A family with three [[Youngling/Legends|children]], including the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|girl]] [[Shanna Kinn]], lived there, with Kinn spending [[Time/Legends|time]] with her two older brothers and learning to repair [[Starship/Legends|starships]] as a result.<ref name="Journal 9" />

At some point, [[Imperial Military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrived at Dorriella and occupied it. During an Imperial raid on a suspected [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] hideout, Kinn's family was [[Death/Legends|killed]], leaving the girl an orphan. Subsequently, in order to survive on Dorriella's streets, Kinn resorted to [[Thief/Legends|thievery]] and eventually stowed away aboard a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] that took her off-world.<ref name="Journal 9" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dorriella was mentioned in "[[Fizzi's Slightly Used Starships (article)|Fizzi's Slightly Used Starships]]," a [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article written by [[John J. Richardson III]] and published in the [[February]] [[1996]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 9|ninth issue]] of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine.<ref name="Journal 9" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dorriella system, and therefore Dorriella itself, in grid square I-17.<ref name="TEA" />
